channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: China says US has 'gravely backpedaled' on Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's Foreign Ministry took issue Monday with a revised U.S. government fact sheet that removed a line on American opposition to independence for Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gabby Petito told her ex she was scared to leave Brian Laundrie but wanted to, just before murder: new doc
item.description: Days before her murder in Wyoming, Gabby Petito told her ex she was planning to leave Brian Laundrie but was afraid of what he might do, docuseries reveals
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane catches fire at air show in Texas, 1 person injured: reports
item.description: An airplane caught on fire during an air show in Laredo, Texas, on Sunday, leaving one person injured in critical condition. The FAA is investigating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: College student Jaren Barajas beats Damian Lillard in 3-point shooting contest, wins $100K
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jaren Barajas shoots on his backyard hoop maybe once a month these days. He's a busy 18-year-old college student now, so basketball has taken a backburner to academics.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Trump administration cuts reach FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: The Trump administration’s effort to slash the size of the federal workforce reached the Food and Drug Administration this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Obama library, begun with lofty DEI goals, now plagued by $40M racially charged suit, ballooning costs
item.description: Construction of former President Obama's long-awaited library and museum in Chicago began with ambitious plans for diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) but is now plagued by huge cost overruns, delays and a $40 million racially charged lawsuit filed by a minority contractor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Your closed Party City may soon turn into one of these familiar stores
item.description: What used to be your local Party City store may soon be selling books, recliner chairs, cowboy boots or discount merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Scientists are racing to discover the depth of ocean damage sparked by the LA wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — On a recent Sunday, Tracy Quinn drove down the Pacific Coast Highway to assess damage wrought upon the coastline by the Palisades Fire. The water line was darkened by ash. Burnt remnants of washing machines and dryers and metal appliances were strewn about the shoreline.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: U.S. states seem to mark Presidents Day with as many different names and honors as there have been presidents.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Missouri community supports Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: The new NBA All-Star format is here, and Sunday night will show whether it works
item.description: This will be an actual All-Star matchup. On one side, there's a team with 86 combined All-Star selections, $2.7 billion in on-court earnings and where every single player on the team is either an NBA champion, an Olympic gold medalist, or both.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani throws 1st BP of spring training, taking a step toward possibly pitching
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani threw his first bullpen of spring training on Saturday, taking a step toward possibly pitching for the first time since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 20% down? The myth that could be holding home buyers back
item.description: Scraping together a down payment can be challenging in today’s housing market, but lower down payment can leave you with more on hand.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, NewsNation

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tours Boeing plane to highlight aircraft maker's delay in delivering a new Air Force One
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump toured a Boeing airplane to check out new hardware and technology features and highlight the aircraft maker's delay in delivering updated versions of the Air Force One presidential aircraft, the White House said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: Officials with the Flagler County Sheriff's Office are expected to remain active in the area for several days as recovery operations continue.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: NASCAR disqualifies Kligerman for too-low truck after flag-planting win at Daytona. He is appealing
item.description: Full-time television analyst and part-time NASCAR driver Parker Kligerman claimed the checkered flag for the Truck Series season opener and promptly planted it in the grass by the Daytona International Speedway logo.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: First homes and weddings have never been more expensive
item.description: Weddings and homeownership are two major goals for many couples, but affording both takes years of careful saving and planning.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Getler, KRON4

item.title: Trump Media says it lost more than $400 million last year
item.description: The parent company of President Donald Trump’s social networking site Truth Social says it lost $400.9 million last year and its annual revenue declined 12% to $3.6 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The White House barred a credentialed Associated Press reporter and photographer from boarding the presidential airplane Friday for a weekend trip with Donald Trump, saying the news agency’s stance on how to refer to the Gulf of Mexico was to blame for the exclusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Anger, chaos and confusion take hold as federal workers face mass layoffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Workers across the country responded with anger and confusion Friday as they grappled with the Trump administration 's aggressive effort to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off probationary employees who have yet to qualify for civil service protec
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, BRIAN WITTE, MIKE HOUSEHOLDER and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House limits AP access to Oval Office, Air Force One indefinitely
item.description: The White House says it will limit Associated Press journalists' access to the Oval Office and Air Force One, an escalation of a brooding conflict between the Trump administration and the wire service this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: NYC mayor to allow immigration officials to operate at Rikers jail after Trump border czar meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he’ll allow federal immigration officials to operate at the city’s Rikers Island jail following a meeting Thursday with President Donald Trump’s border czar. Adams said he’ll issue an executive order reestablishing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC
